title: Prosthechea fuertesii

This species belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae, a large and diverse group of orchids. Prosthechea fuertesii is further classified within the tribe Cymbidieae, which is a group of orchids commonly known as "bug orchids." The genus Prosthechea itself is a relatively small group of orchids, with only a few species known to exist. Prosthechea fuertesii is native to the tropical regions of Central America.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
